title: orthilia secunda subsp. kareliniana
intro: Orthilia secunda subsp. kareliniana, also known as Karelin's Orthilia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Orthilia secunda subsp. kareliniana include its ability to help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
